nodes.name: Daily 09:00 (catch-up)
nodes.notes: Safety net AFTER the 00:30 pipeline: cloud_backup.sh is idempotent (rclone sync moves only changed files), so if the pipeline ran, this is a no-op costing seconds. If the Mac slept through the wake window — the recurring failure mode — this is the run that actually happens.

nodes.name: Ingress: cloud_backup.sh → Dropbox
nodes.notes: Syncs market_cache, bhavcopy_cache, cache_seed, gmd cache_seed and ~/data (duckdb) to dropbox:market-data-archive/current with dated history/ versioning; weekly Monday pg_dump. Exits non-zero on any tree failure, which fails this execution loudly. Detail log: ~/market-pipeline/code/python_files/cloud_backup.log

nodes.name: GATE: mirror verified?
nodes.notes: INDEPENDENT per-tree check: rclone size of each current/<tree> vs local du with the same excludes (10% tolerance for legitimate churn between backup and verify), plus pg-dump recency (<8d). Replaces a whole-mirror byte count, which a half-synced tree passes. Gates measure the world, not the process — same principle as marketpipeline001's ingest gate.

parameters.command: =cd /Users/umashankar/market-pipeline/code/python_files && ./.venv/bin/python3 send_alert.py "n8n dropbox backup: {{ $json.error || $json.stdout || 'backup or verification failed' }}"

nodes.name: ALERT
nodes.notes: Reuses send_alert.py so n8n alerts and shell-run alerts look identical in the inbox. Without this, a failed backup only dies quietly in the executions list — the exact silent-staleness failure mode the whole 2026-07-22 repair cycle was about.
